skill.name: science-popularization-article
skill.description: Generates science popularization articles with titles and outlines based on medical topics and style preferences. Invoke when user needs to create medical/science popular content for public education.
skill.summary: Generates science popularization articles with professionally crafted titles and structured outlines for medical and health topics.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 25 static analysis findings were evaluated and determined to be FALSE POSITIVES. The 7 high-risk 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts match non-code text in markdown documentation, JSON audit data, and an argparse description string. The 16 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' alerts are markdown code formatting (inline backticks and fenced code blocks) showing bash commands for documentation purposes. The 'Hardcoded URL' is a legitimate source attribution link. The 'System reconnaissance' alert matches a quality-control instruction about reproducible output. No prompt injection attempts were detected. This is a legitimate skill for generating science popularization articles with no confirmed security concerns.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Static analyzer reported 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at 5 locations. All are false positives: SKILL.md:3 and SKILL.md:19 contain the skill description in YAML front matter and feature summary; SKILL.md:98 and SKILL.md:111 contain input requirements and output contract text; scripts/validate_skill.py:12 is an argparse help string; science-popularization-article_audit_result_v2.json:77 is an audit note about maintainability scoring. No actual cryptographic code exists at any of these locations.

high_findings.title: System Reconnaissance (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Static analyzer reported 'System reconnaissance' at SKILL.md:46. The content is: 'Output discipline: keep results reproducible, identify assumptions explicitly, and avoid undocumented side effects.' This is a legitimate quality-control instruction for reproducible output, not system reconnaissance.

medium_findings.title: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution (FALSE POSITIVE)
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer reported 16 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. All instances are standard markdown code formatting. Inline backticks format code references like `scripts/validate_skill.py` and `python scripts/validate_skill.py`. Fenced code blocks (lines 28-32, 52-54) document bash commands for user reference. These are documentation conventions, not shell command execution via backtick operators.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer reported 'Hardcoded URL' at SKILL.md:7. The URL is a legitimate source attribution link to the GitHub repository (https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). This is standard open-source attribution, not data exfiltration or malicious network activity.

content.user_title: Generate Science Popularization Articles from Medical Topics
content.value_statement: Medical professionals and science communicators need to translate complex research into accessible content for public education. This skill generates structured science popularization articles with professionally crafted titles and outlines based on any medical or health topic.
actual_capabilities: Generates 3 candidate article titles optimized for the selected writing style
Creates detailed article outlines with section-by-section prompts
Writes complete science popularization articles of approximately 2500 words
Supports both easy-to-understand and professional-authoritative writing styles
Produces content suitable for hospital public education, social media, and awareness campaigns
limitations: Does not generate peer-reviewed research papers or academic publications
Does not include original scientific research or unpublished lab data
Style quality depends on clarity of the provided medical topic description
Factual accuracy depends entirely on the source material provided as input

use_cases.title: Hospital Public Education Content
use_cases.description: Generate accessible health articles for hospital waiting areas, patient newsletters, and community outreach programs.

use_cases.title: Medical Company Social Media
use_cases.description: Create engaging science content for pharmaceutical or medical device company social media channels and blogs.

use_cases.title: Public Health Awareness Campaigns
use_cases.description: Produce clear science communication materials for government or NGO public health initiatives and community education.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Article Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a science popularization article about [medical topic] in [easy-to-understand / professional] style.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick article creation with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Title-First Collaborative Approach
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a science article about [topic]. First, suggest 3 titles in [style] style. I will select one. Then create the full outline and article based on my choice.
prompt_templates.scenario: Collaborative title selection before full article writing

prompt_templates.title: Style-Specific Detailed Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a science popularization article. Topic: [medical topic]. Target audience: [specific audience]. Style: [easy-to-understand or professional]. Include an introduction, 3 to 5 main sections with explanations, and a conclusion. Word count target: [specify].
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed brief with specific style and audience targeting

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Article Series Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a series of 3 science popularization articles about [broad topic area]. Suggest 3 connected subtopics with proposed titles for each. After I approve the plan, generate the first article in [style] style with a full outline.
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning a multi-article series on a complex medical topic

output_examples.input: Topic: mRNA vaccine technology. Style: Easy-to-understand.
output_examples.output:
3 Candidate Titles:
1. How mRNA Vaccines Train Your Immune System: A Simple Guide
2. The Messenger in Your Medicine: Understanding mRNA Vaccines
3. From Lab to Arm: The Journey of mRNA Vaccine Development

Selected Title: How mRNA Vaccines Train Your Immune System: A Simple Guide

Outline:
- Introduction: Why mRNA vaccines represent a breakthrough
- Section 1: What is mRNA and how does it work in your cells?
- Section 2: From discovery to vaccine: The development process
- Section 3: Safety monitoring and real-world effectiveness
- Conclusion: The future of mRNA technology beyond COVID-19

output_examples.input: Topic: Gut microbiome and mental health. Style: Professional-authoritative.
output_examples.output:
3 Candidate Titles:
1. The Gut-Brain Axis: Emerging Evidence for Microbiome-Mediated Neurological Effects
2. Enteric Microbiology and Mental Health: A Review of Current Research
3. Microbial Modulation of Neurotransmitter Pathways: Clinical Implications

Selected Title: The Gut-Brain Axis: Emerging Evidence for Microbiome-Mediated Neurological Effects

Outline:
- Introduction: Historical context and current research landscape
- Section 1: Mechanisms of gut-brain communication
- Section 2: Clinical studies linking microbiome composition to mood disorders
- Section 3: Therapeutic interventions targeting the microbiome
- Conclusion: Limitations and future research directions

best_practices: Provide a specific medical topic with clear context to get the most relevant article structure
Select the writing style that matches your target audience before generating content
Review and refine the generated outline before proceeding to full article generation
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for generating peer-reviewed research papers or clinical trial reports
Do not request articles on topics without providing sufficient background or source material
Do not expect original scientific claims or unpublished research data in the output

faq.question: What writing styles does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports two styles: easy-to-understand (using metaphors and simple language for the general public) and professional-authoritative (formal terminology and structured logic for professionals).

faq.question: How long is the generated article?
faq.answer: The skill generates articles of approximately 2500 words, including a selected title, structured outline, and full article content.

faq.question: Can I generate articles in languages other than English?
faq.answer: The skill works with the language of your input. Provide your topic and style preference in the desired language for best results.

faq.question: Does this skill verify scientific accuracy?
faq.answer: No. The skill structures content based on the information you provide. Factual accuracy depends on the source material you give as input.

faq.question: Can I use this for academic publications?
faq.answer: No. This skill is designed for science popularization and public education content, not for peer-reviewed academic papers or formal research publications.

faq.question: How do I choose between the two writing styles?
faq.answer: Use easy-to-understand style for general public audiences and social media. Use professional-authoritative style for educated readers, medical professionals, or formal communication contexts.
